Use.title: Ship a four-slide deck. Yours.
Use.lede: Use everything from parts 1-4. You have one hour. The goal is not a beautiful deck — the goal is a deck whose styles, templates, and master you can defend in code review.

Use.title: Pick a topic that is not 'about builder'.
Use.body: A four-slide deck on something you actually care about — a hobby, a side project, a city. The constraint forces the design to argue with real content.

Use.title: Build it bottom-up: styles → master → templates → slides.
Use.body: No copy-pasting between slides. If two slides need the same layout, that's a template. If the template needs the same chrome, that's a master.
Use.deliverable: A built .pptx + your three source files (styles, master, page template).

Use.label: REVIEW PAIRINGS
Use.body: Pair with the person to your left. Five minutes per person — they read your XML aloud and you watch where they hesitate. Hesitation is the design feedback.

VStack.Text: We covered four primitives — containers, styles, templates, masters — and built a deck end-to-end. You now have the same toolkit the @slideglance/builder reference deck uses; the only difference is scale.
